pages.text: Everyone knows that Khuzwayo loves to dance. When the village leader chooses him to be the next Ingoma dancer, no one is surprised.
Ingoma is a dance to celebrate the first crops at the beginning of the year. Khuzwayo’s family is proud.
But Khuzwayo is worried. How will he dance in front of so many people from so many villages? He normally only performs for a few neighbours.
“I can’t do it! It’s too scary.” He runs away...
Everyone wonders where he is. No one can find him. But Khuzwayo’s best friends know exactly where he would be!
“I am nervous. What if I make a mistake?”
“We can help you. “Khuzwayo, you’re so good. You’re Let’s all practise together!” ready to dance with the older dancers!”
On the day of the Ingoma dance Khuzwayo leads his village to the arena wearing a special outfit. He still feels a little nervous, but the rattles on his feet guide him.
He stomps his legs. He waves his arms. Everyone loves Khuzwayo’s dance!
